signals.headline: March 2026 inflation confirmed at 2
signals.raw: March 2026 inflation confirmed at 2.16-2.2% year-over-year**, with the latest official reading released April 2, 2026[1][3]. South Korea's inflation rose from 2.0% in February, driven primarily by rising global oil prices stemming from the U.S.-Iran conflict[1][4].

signals.headline: Forecasts have been revised upward** due to energy cost pressures: AMRO raised its 2026 inflation forecast to 2
signals.raw: Forecasts have been revised upward** due to energy cost pressures: AMRO raised its 2026 inflation forecast to 2.3% (up 0.4 percentage points from March's 1.9% forecast)[2], while Morgan Stanley projects inflation could spike to 2.8% in the second quarter[5].
